sections.title: In-flight familiarisation syllabus
sections.body:
ENERAL CONSIDERATIONS:

CAUTION
There is NO requirement to land from the familiarisation approach, a low approach and go-around will fulfil the
familiarisation requirement.

FAMILIARISATION FLIGHT:
CREW COMPOSITION
MINIMA
REQUIREMENTS

DAY ONLY
Ceiling 2500ft
Visibility 5km

•

operating to LESO.

as SIC to Commander who is

PM.

NTA-TRN-FCM-3104-FLT-TR-ALL and send it to you for your records.

sections.title: Limitations / Miscellaneous
sections.body:
Aerodrome qualification is valid for 12 months.
• If the last flight into LESO is more than 12 months ago, then the initial qualification will require to be performed
again.
• PF shall hold a current AHQ.

RWY 04 Only:
• PAPI set to 4.75 degrees so landing RWY 04 is a STEEP APPROACH.
• Steep approach approved aircraft and qualified crew shall be used.
• Steep approach procedures and performance shall be used.
• Fractional Ops performance is PROHIBITED.

Mountain airport - NO NIGHT CIRCLING Landing RWY 04 is DAY ONLY.

Limitations to the use of Jeppesen Published Minima and Speeds for Circling to RWY 04:
Protection areas used for the design of the circling minima at LESO are considerably less than those prescribed by
PANS-OPS and the limiting speeds published by Jeppesen are NOT compatible with these reduced areas, therefore
NetJets aircrafts are limited to circling in accordance with the limitations, minima and procedures shown in 9.4
CIRCLING TO RWY 04 ONLY.

sections.title: Overview
sections.body:
he airfield lies on the north-western part of Spain next to the French border about 14nm southwest of LFBZ (Biarritz).
High terrain surrounds the aerodrome.

AIRPORT OVERVIEW

lines: he airfield lies on the north-western part of Spain next to the French border about 14nm southwest of LFBZ (Biarritz).

sections.title: Operational procedures — General
sections.body:
ESO is categorised by NetJets as a CAT-C Aerodrome, as a result a previous real approach in VMC or in an approved
simulator is required.

lines: ESO is categorised by NetJets as a CAT-C Aerodrome, as a result a previous real approach in VMC or in an approved

sections.title: Arrival procedures
sections.body:
.1 GENERAL
Preference should be given to the RNP approaches which provide a straight in approach to both RWY 22 and 04.
The VOR RWY 22 approach and Circling RWY 04 should only be used as a backup in the event the RNP approaches
are not available (e.g. aircraft equipment unserviceability or GPS jamming).

9.2 PREFERED APPROACHES
RWY 22: RNP Z/Y/X RWY 22 (Chart 12-1,-2 and -3)
RWY 04: RNP A (Chart 12-4)
• Straight in approach to RWY 04 - As per Jeppesen.

SAN SEBASTIAN, SPAIN
SAN SEBASTIAN
10-10-2P4

-
9.3 BACKUP APPROACHES
Circling RWY 04 (9.4 Circling to RWY 04)
• Circling from RNP Z/Y/X RWY 22 and VOR RWY 22.
VOR RWY 22 (Chart 13-1)
• Straight in approach to RWY 22: As per Jeppesen.

Final RWY 04

FINAL RWY 22

SAN SEBASTIAN, SPAIN
SAN SEBASTIAN
10-10-2P5

-
9.4. CIRCLING TO RWY 04
• PAPI set to 4.75 degrees so landing RWY 04 is a STEEP APPROACH.
• Steep approach approved aircraft and qualified crew shall be used.
• Steep approach procedures and performance shall be used.
• Fractional Ops performance is PROHIBITED.
NetJets Minima for all circling approaches to RWY 04:
• Visibility: 5km;
• Ceiling: 2400ft;
• MDA (H): 2350ft (2335ft);
• Max Speed: 160KT IAS;

Guidance for circling from VOR RWY 22 to landing RWY 04:
The following guidance is based on a speed of 160 kt. If a lower speed is used, then the required bank angle
and ROD will reduce. However, to ensure a stabilised approach is possible, it is recommended to keep to the
ground track shown.
If circling to RWY 04 from RNP Z/Y/X RWY 22, join this procedure at an appropriate point (e.g. breakoff or
beginning of downwind) and then follow the same procedure from that point.
• Prior to starting the approach, check with LESO TWR for permission to enter Danger Area “LE(D)–8” as the
circling will enter this area and advise them you will be circling at 2350 ft AMSL.

SAN SEBASTIAN, SPAIN
SAN SEBASTIAN
10-10-2P6

-
• Note that 2350 ft is above the platform altitude for the VOR RWY 22, do not descend to platform altitude,
maintain 2350 ft.

• At D5.0 “SSN” (Missed Approach Point), if visual reference for circling is acquired turn right track 264. Maintain
2350 ft.

• Maintain track 264. until reaching the ridge, then turn left onto a downwind track 219. Maintain 2350 ft.
• Follow the ridge until approaching the mast (mast is 1931 ft amsl), approx. radial 335. from “SSN” VOR. Maintain
2350 ft.

SAN SEBASTIAN, SPAIN
SAN SEBASTIAN
10-10-2P7

-

• Initiate a left turn using 25° AOB and when established turning away from the mast & high ground, initiate
descent initially to follow a 5.5° path (1560 fpm at 160 kt). Max speed - 160 kts, Min speed - Vref (steep
approach) plus 10 kts.

CAUTION:
Do not be tempted to turn significantly earlier as this will require a very high rate of descent to intercept the 4.75°
PAPI and perform a stabilised approach.

SAN SEBASTIAN, SPAIN
SAN SEBASTIAN
10-10-2P8

-
• Maintain the descent throughout the turn to roll out on final approx. 1000 ft / 1.87 NM from the threshold
on the 4.75° PAPI (adjust descent rate in the turn to achieve this).
• Reduce the RoD and speed to maintain the 4.75° PAPI for a stabilised ‘Steep Approach’ landing.

SAN SEBASTIAN, SPAIN
SAN SEBASTIAN
10-10-2P9

-

9.5. CIRCUIT DIRECTION
Circuit pattern is to the North / West: RWY 22 right hand circuits, RWY 04 left hand.

are not available (e.g. aircraft equipment unserviceability or GPS jamming).
Straight in approach to RWY 04 - As per Jeppesen.
Circling from RNP Z/Y/X RWY 22 and VOR RWY 22.
Straight in approach to RWY 22: As per Jeppesen.
PAPI set to 4.75 degrees so landing RWY 04 is a STEEP APPROACH.
Steep approach approved aircraft and qualified crew shall be used.
Steep approach procedures and performance shall be used.

sections.title: Changes since the last revision
sections.body:
he briefing was updated to reflect the new RNP Index charts and numbering. A caution note regarding
the familiarisation requirement has also been highlighted.
Provide feedback to Lis-OpsTechnical@netjets.com
